# Scale AI Dialect (SGP)

> AI Registry entry [AIR-69](https://linear.app/janusd/issue/AIR-69/scale-ai-dialect-sgp) — status **Backlog** as of 2026-04-02. Departments: —.

**Category:** Enterprise AI Platform / Agentic Infrastructure
**Status:** Backlog
**Cost:** Custom enterprise (demo/sales required)
**Departments:** Technology, AI Policy

## Overview

Self-improving intelligence layer within Scale's GenAI Platform (SGP) — Scale's end-to-end infrastructure for building, evaluating, deploying enterprise AI agents. Announced 31 March 2026. Captures how org domain experts make decisions and encodes that reasoning into living systems for AI agents. NOT standalone — core capability of SGP. SGP customers: Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Global Atlantic, TIME.

## Key Capabilities

* AI-Native Data Layer — connects siloed enterprise data into semantic system
* Living Knowledge Store — tribal knowledge + expert decision logic encoded
* Enterprise Evaluations — domain-specific eval suites
* **VeRO** (Versioning, Rewards, Observation) — autonomous agent optimisation
* **Enterprise Decision Record (EDR)** — auditable trace for every agent output
* Agent Execution — enterprise-grade hosting/orchestration
* Agent Operations — telemetry, monitoring
* Model Portability — model-independent, swap models without losing institutional intelligence

## Security

* Deploys in customer VPC (cloud-agnostic)
* Designed for regulated environments (healthcare, financial, legal)
* Auditable decision trails (EDR)
* AWS partnership

## Janus Relevance

Addresses core challenge for consultancies: capturing institutional knowledge so AI agents reason like experienced staff. Dialect's expert decision logic encoding could help Janus build AI workflows reflecting specific methodologies. Auditable decision records align with §5.3.1 human-in-the-loop oversight.

## Considerations

* Not standalone — Dialect is capability within broader SGP platform
* Enterprise sales engagement required
* Integration complexity — VPC deployment
* Vendor lock-in risk (platform commitment)
* Multi-country data residency needs confirmation

*Backlog.*
